title: Library
listing_description: The library comprises a major art and architecture research collection of print titles, auction catalogs, serial subscriptions, and extensive digital collections.
short_description: The Ryerson and Burnham Libraries constitute a major art and architecture research collection serving the Art Institute of Chicago and scholars in the fields of art and architectural history with more than 500,000 print titles, 100,000 auction catalogs, 200 current serial subscriptions, and extensive digital collections.
copy: LOCATION AND PUBLIC HOURS The library is located to the south of the Grand Staircase inside the museum’s Michigan Avenue entrance. Although the Art Institute of Chicago's galleries are now open , the Ryerson & Burnham Libraries' reading room remains closed to all researchers until further notice. Remote reference services will be restored on April 19, 2021. Staff are continuing to work a staggered schedule, so the best way to reach us with your questions is to email the Archives or email the Libraries with your inquiries. We are diligently working toward the restoration of in-person research access and the delivery of new and enhanced services for all of our patrons. In order to accommodate these improvements, current turnaround times for remote reference services are: 6 weeks for general reference questions. 6 weeks for imaging requests of existing images . 12 weeks for imaging requests requiring new images. We will resume interlibrary loan services on May 3, 2021. Thank you for your patience as we work to restore access and services.   CONNECT WITH US You can learn more about the Ryerson & Burnham Libraries and our collections on our social media accounts: Facebook Instagram Tumblr
